concept.title: Preface: Who This Guide Is For
concept.shortdesc: A progressive training guide that takes you from first DITA topic to production publishing and AI-powered delivery — with hands-on tutorials, real examples, and measurable outcomes for both technical writers and software engineers.

section.title: Who should read this guide?
section.p: This guide serves two primary learning tracks — you can follow one or both:

ul.li: Technical writers — Master DITA authoring, information typing, reuse, metadata, and publishing workflows. You will write real topics, build maps, and publish multi-channel output.

Software engineers — Master DITA-OT architecture, plugin development, XSLT customization, CI/CD pipelines, Docker builds, and AI integration. You will build plugins, automate publishing, and connect content to RAG pipelines.

section.p: The guide is also valuable for content strategists planning modernization initiatives, documentation managers evaluating structured content ROI, and information architects designing content models.

section.title: What you will learn
section.p: The guide follows a progressive learning path — each part builds on the previous one. By the end, you will be able to:

ol.li: Explain why unstructured content creates real problems for your team — duplication, inconsistency, scaling failure — and how structured content solves them
Set up a DITA authoring environment with DITA-OT, an XML editor, and project scaffolding
Author concept, task, and reference topics using proper information typing, and apply reuse patterns (conref, keyref, conkeyref)
Design topic hierarchies, controlled vocabulary, and maps that scale
Enrich content with metadata, taxonomy, and Schema.org markup for both human findability and AI readability
Publish HTML5, PDF, and multi-channel output with DITAVAL filtering and automated pipelines
Develop DITA-OT plugins, customize HTML5 transforms with XSLT, and deploy via Docker and CI/CD
Connect enriched DITA content to RAG pipelines, vector stores, and chatbot delivery
Measure the ROI and impact of intelligent content across your organization

section.title: How this guide is organized
section.p: The guide has 10 parts organized in a progressive learning path. Parts build on each other, so work through them in order. XML basics and inline explanations are provided where needed — no prior DITA or XML experience is required.

row.entry: Problems your team faces today with unstructured content; what intelligent content is; AI readiness maturity model; unstructured format limitations
DITA fundamentals and XML syntax primer; install DITA-OT; configure your editor; project setup and quick start
Topic types and authoring foundations; hands-on WidgetPro tutorial with inline tips; information typing basics; information typing for AI; reuse and variant management
Topic-based architecture; taxonomy and controlled vocabulary; progressive map design
Metadata enrichment; Schema.org integration; migration from unstructured XML (step-by-step); AI-ready authoring guide; before-and-after examples
HTML5 and PDF builds; multi-channel output; DITA project files; CI/CD publishing pipelines; troubleshooting
OT mental model and preprocessing pipeline; plugin creation; Docker containers; GitHub Actions CI/CD; debugging
Hands-on labs: XSLT overrides, sidebar TOC, element styling, production patterns, plugin packaging
RAG pipeline architecture; chunking and embedding; retrieval and generation
ROI measurement; utilizing intelligent features; impact on final products

section.p: Each part includes transition sections ("What's Next") that explain how the current material connects to what follows, so you always know where you are in the learning path.
